---
title: "Files and Data"
description: "Read, write, validate, and recover from problems when programs interact with data outside memory."
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Why this chapter matters

Read, write, validate, and recover from problems when programs interact with data outside memory.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.questions}
### Questions

- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Paths and Text Files](paths-and-text-files.qmd) — Find files reliably and read or write plain text safely.
- [Structured Files](structured-files.qmd) — Read and write CSV and JSON data formats.
- [Exceptions and Validation](exceptions-and-validation.qmd) — Handle expected failures and validate user input before trusting it.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "files-and-data-overview",
  "title": "Files and Data overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Files and Data chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Read, write, validate, and recover from problems when programs interact with data outside memory.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Read, write, validate, and recover from problems when programs interact with data outside memory."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
